apps.name: Slack
apps.description: Slack desktop storage on macOS, mainly Electron cache, logs, and local browser-style state.
warnings: Do not delete IndexedDB, Local Storage, or other database-like folders unless you are intentionally resetting local app state.
Close Slack before manually touching cache or log folders.

paths.description: Electron HTTP and runtime cache.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Usually lower risk than database or session folders.
paths.warning: Review the exact folder and close Slack first.

paths.description: Generated code cache used by Electron.
GPU-related rendering cache.
Service worker cache storage.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Lower risk than IndexedDB or Local Storage.
paths.warning: Do not generalize this hint to the whole Service Worker folder.

paths.description: Slack logs and diagnostics.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Logs are usually safer to review and prune than live app state.

paths.description: Browser-style local databases for app state and offline data.
paths.warning: High risk for workspace state and cached content. Do not delete by default.

paths.description: Local storage for session and app state.
paths.warning: May contain session, account, or preference state.

apps.name: Notion
apps.description: Notion desktop storage on macOS, including cache and local app data.
warnings: The full Application Support/Notion folder can include local app data, not only disposable cache.
Close Notion before manually cleaning cache-like directories.

paths.description: Primary runtime cache.
Generated code cache.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Usually lower risk than databases or app state.

paths.description: GPU rendering cache.
Local database files used by the app.
paths.warning: May contain offline workspace state. Do not delete by default.

paths.description: App or updater cache files under the macOS Caches directory.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Cache folder is a lower-risk review candidate than Application Support.

paths.description: ShipIt updater state and logs.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Mostly useful for update diagnostics rather than daily use.
paths.warning: Keep it if you are troubleshooting update problems.

apps.name: WeChat
apps.description: WeChat media/cache usage across account directories on macOS. Supports both classic container layout and newer xwechat_files layout.
warnings: This rule focuses on media/cache-like directories under each account root, not the full message database.
Check Favorites and transferred files before deleting anything manually.

paths.description: Classic WeChat macOS account roots. Scans per-account Cache, predownload, mmxpt, Files, Video, Favorites and related temp/media directories.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Cache, predownload and mmxpt are usually safer to clean than Files, Video or Favorites.
paths.warning: Do not assume all media is disposable. Files, Favorites and message-related directories may contain important content.

paths.description: Newer WeChat macOS storage root. Scans per-account msg/attach, msg/file and msg/video plus common cache-like directories when present.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Review large files under msg/attach, msg/file and msg/video before deleting; they may be downloaded chat media.
paths.warning: This root may contain valuable downloaded files and media. Back up important content first.

apps.name: Feishu
apps.description: Feishu local shell storage on macOS. Publicly documented paths are less granular than some other apps, so this rule stays conservative.
warnings: The LarkShell data directory can mix cache, logs, and live local state.
Prefer reviewing the log folder first; avoid deleting the whole LarkShell tree unless you are intentionally resetting Feishu.

paths.description: Feishu diagnostic logs under sdk_storage.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Logs are usually safer to review and prune than the full data directory.

paths.description: Main Feishu local storage directory documented in reset guides.
paths.warning: This may include active local state, downloaded content, and cache. Do not delete by default.

apps.name: Adobe Lightroom Classic
apps.description: Default Lightroom Classic locations on macOS. Adobe notes that some of these paths can be customized, so the rule focuses on standard defaults.
warnings: Catalog and catalog-adjacent files can be critical. Do not delete .lrcat, .lock, or .lrcat-journal files casually.
Preview caches are usually safer to rebuild than catalogs, presets, or backups.

paths.description: Default preview cache next to the catalog.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Adobe community guidance says preview caches can be rebuilt automatically.

paths.description: Default smart preview cache next to the catalog.
paths.warning: Smaller than normal previews but still related to editing workflow. Review before deleting.

paths.description: Default Lightroom Classic catalog backups.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Review old dated backups manually; keep the recent ones you still need.

paths.description: Presets, templates, and support data.
paths.warning: May contain presets and other user configuration, not just disposable cache.

paths.description: Camera Raw settings and converted preset data.
paths.warning: Settings and presets are user data, not cleanup-first targets.

apps.name: Microsoft Teams
apps.description: Microsoft Teams storage on macOS, covering both classic and newer container-based app layouts.
warnings: Teams cache paths differ between classic and new Teams.
Support log archives are usually created in Downloads, but this rule does not scan ~/Downloads because it is global user storage rather than app-owned storage.

paths.description: Classic Teams local data and browser-style cache location.
paths.warning: Classic Teams path can mix cache, logs, and active local state.

paths.description: Classic Teams cache directory.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Usually a lower-risk review target than the full Application Support tree.

paths.description: New Teams container on macOS.
paths.warning: New Teams stores active local state here; do not delete by default.

paths.description: New Teams group container storage.
paths.warning: Shared container may include cached files and live app state.

apps.name: BaiduNetdisk
apps.description: BaiduNetdisk storage on macOS. Public sources consistently mention caches, preferences, and support files, but publicly documented path detail is weaker than for Slack or Notion.
warnings: Downloaded files may live in a user-chosen sync or download folder outside the app-owned Library paths and are not covered by default.
Treat Application Support data as higher risk than pure cache.

paths.description: BaiduNetdisk cache directory commonly referenced in macOS cleanup guides.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Cache folder is the lower-risk place to review first.

paths.description: BaiduNetdisk support files and local app data.
paths.warning: May include sync state or downloaded metadata; do not delete by default.

paths.description: Possible BaiduNetdisk log folder.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: Logs are usually safer to review than live app state.

apps.name: Telegram
apps.description: Telegram for macOS local storage, with the main footprint typically under its group container.
warnings: This rule measures only stable/*/postbox/media. It does not count Telegram databases or other container data.
Deleting postbox/media can force media to re-download later.

paths.description: Scan each Telegram account under stable and sum only postbox/media.
paths.safe_to_clean_hint: If you manually clear postbox/media, close Telegram first. Expect media to re-download when opened again.
paths.warning: Do not delete postbox/db or the whole stable account directory.
